name: 北京
description: Beijing, the capital of China, is a city where ancient history meets modern dynamism. Home to the Forbidden City, Great Wall, and countless imperial treasures, it offers an unparalleled journey through 3,000 years of Chinese civilization. Today, Beijing blends traditional hutong neighborhoods with gleaming skyscrapers, world-class cuisine, and a thriving arts scene.
highlights: Great Wall - One of the Seven Wonders
Temple of Heaven - UNESCO World Heritage

climate.tips: Best visited in spring (April-May) or autumn (September-October) for pleasant weather. Summers are hot and humid, winters cold and dry. Spring often sees sandstorms. Pack layers for variable temperatures.

attractions.name: 故宫博物院
attractions.description: The former imperial palace complex, home to 24 emperors over 500 years. Contains 980 buildings and over 1 million cultural artifacts.
attractions.tips: Book tickets online in advance. Arrive early to avoid crowds. Audio guides available in multiple languages. Wear comfortable shoes.

attractions.name: 八达岭长城
attractions.description: The most visited section of the Great Wall, offering spectacular views and well-preserved fortifications.
attractions.tips: Avoid weekends if possible. Take a guided tour for better historical context. Bring water and snacks. The wall is steep - wear good walking shoes.

attractions.name: 天坛
attractions.description: A Ming dynasty religious complex where emperors prayed for good harvests. UNESCO World Heritage site with stunning circular architecture.
attractions.tips: Best visited in early morning when locals practice tai chi. The echo wall is fascinating - try whispering at different points.

attractions.name: 颐和园
attractions.description: The largest royal garden in the world, featuring the stunning Kunming Lake and Long Corridor with painted scenes.
attractions.tips: Rent a boat on the lake for a different perspective. The Long Corridor has over 14,000 paintings. Visit the tea house for authentic Chinese tea experience.

attractions.name: 天安门广场
attractions.description: The world's largest public square, surrounded by important government buildings and cultural monuments.
attractions.tips: Bring passport for security check. Visit early morning for flag-raising ceremony at sunrise.

attractions.name: 798艺术区
attractions.description: A former military factory turned into Beijing's premier contemporary art hub, featuring galleries, studios, cafes, and street art.
attractions.tips: Best on weekends when more galleries open. Combine with nearby 751 D-Park for industrial heritage. Great for souvenir photos.

attractions.name: 雍和宫
attractions.description: The largest and most well-preserved Tibetan Buddhist temple in Beijing, with stunning architecture and 18-meter Buddha statue.
attractions.tips: One of Beijing's most active temples. Arrive early to experience monk chanting. Best visited in morning for fewer crowds.

attractions.name: 北海公园
attractions.description: A historic imperial garden featuring the iconic White Dagoba, beautiful lakes, and traditional pavilions.
attractions.tips: Walk around the lake and up to the White Pagoda for classic Beijing views. Combine with nearby Houhai bar area.

attractions.name: 慕田峪长城
attractions.description: A fully restored section with 22 watchtowers, offering a less crowded alternative to Badaling with cable car access.
attractions.tips: Less crowded than Badaling. Book a tour or take Bus 916 from Dongzhimen. Bring layers as it is windy at the top.

attractions.name: 梨园剧场
attractions.description: Experience traditional Chinese opera with face-changing, martial arts, and singing at this dedicated theater.
attractions.tips: Book through hotel concierge or travel agency. Photos allowed before the show but not during. Great cultural introduction.

attractions.name: 南锣鼓巷
attractions.description: A historic hutong alley with trendy shops, street food, bars, and traditional courtyard residences.
attractions.tips: Visit in the evening for atmosphere. Explore side alleys for authentic hutong life. Try fried rice cake and sugar paintings.

attractions.name: 中国国家博物馆
attractions.description: The largest museum in China, showcasing 3,000 years of Chinese civilization with over 1.5 million cultural artifacts.
attractions.tips: Book tickets online 1 week in advance. Arrive early. Best combined with Tiananmen Square visit.

attractions.name: 中山公园
attractions.description: A historic imperial garden turned public park, featuring ancient cypress trees, traditional pavilions, and seasonal flower displays.
attractions.address: West of Tiananmen Square, Dongcheng District
attractions.tips: Best visited in spring for cherry blossoms and roses. Great for morning tai chi practice.

attractions.name: 劳动人民文化宫
attractions.description: Former imperial temple dedicated to Confucius and the god of literature, now a cultural venue for workers.
attractions.address: East of Tiananmen Square, Dongcheng District
attractions.tips: Less crowded than major attractions. Combine with a visit to the nearby National Museum.

attractions.name: 景山公园
attractions.description: A 45-meter artificial hill offering the best panoramic views of the Forbidden City and central Beijing.
attractions.tips: Visit at sunrise or sunset for stunning photos. The hill is steep but short - climb to the top for the best views.

attractions.name: 恭王府
attractions.description: The largest surviving princely mansion in Beijing, featuring traditional Chinese architecture, beautiful gardens, and Qing dynasty artifacts.
attractions.tips: Book tickets online. Audio guide included. Allow time to explore both the mansion and the garden.

attractions.name: 什刹海
attractions.description: A scenic area of lakes, hutongs, and historic sites including Prince Gong's Mansion. Popular for cycling, boat rides, and exploring traditional Beijing life.
attractions.tips: Best in late afternoon and evening. Rent a bike to explore the hutongs. Visit the bar street after dark.

attractions.name: 钟鼓楼
attractions.description: Medieval timekeeping towers that once governed daily life in Beijing with bell strikes and drum beats marking the hours.
attractions.tips: Watch the drum performance at the top of each hour. Combine with a hutong walk afterward.

attractions.name: 大栅栏
attractions.description: A historic commercial district with traditional Beijing architecture, famous brand shops, and local snacks.
attractions.tips: Great for souvenir shopping. Try traditional snacks like laoming damand and sugar-flour paintings. Walk to nearby Qianmen afterward.

attractions.name: 北京欢乐谷
attractions.description: A large amusement park with roller coasters, water rides, and family-friendly attractions.
attractions.tips: Weekdays are less crowded. Wear comfortable shoes. Bring sunscreen in summer. Lockers available for rent.

attractions.name: 奥林匹克公园
attractions.description: The main venue of the 2008 Beijing Olympics, featuring the Bird's Nest, Water Cube, and beautiful gardens.
attractions.tips: Best visited at night for spectacular lighting. Watch the light show at Water Cube. Combine with nearby national conventions.

attractions.name: 圆明园
attractions.description: The ruins of the Old Summer Palace, destroyed in 1860, now a memorial park with historic foundations and lakes.
attractions.tips: Allow 3 hours to explore. Visit the smaller but significant exhibitions about history. Wear walking shoes.

attractions.name: 北京自然博物馆
attractions.description: Popular natural science museum with dinosaur fossils, wildlife exhibits, and interactive science displays.
attractions.tips: Very popular with families. Book tickets online. Best for children interested in dinosaurs and nature.

attractions.name: 中国电影博物馆
attractions.description: The world's largest film museum with extensive exhibits on Chinese cinema history and interactive displays.
attractions.address: 9 Hua Jia Di Village, Chaoyang District
attractions.tips: One of the largest film museums globally. Great for film enthusiasts. Free entry but registration required.

attractions.name: 西洋楼遗址
attractions.description: The iconic ruins of the Old Summer Palace's European-style palaces, built for Emperor Qianlong by Jesuit missionaries.
attractions.ticketPrice: Included in Old Summer Palace ticket (25¥ full)
attractions.tips: The most photographed part of Old Summer Palace. The marble boat (Hanhuachi) is the most famous ruin.

attractions.name: 麋鹿苑
attractions.description: A nature reserve and conservation center for the milu deer (Pere David's deer), featuring observation platforms and educational exhibits.
attractions.tips: Bring binoculars for wildlife viewing. Best in early morning. Located south of Old Summer Palace.

attractions.name: 北京大学
attractions.description: China's first modern national university, known for its beautiful campus with traditional Chinese architecture and lakes.
attractions.tips: Arrive early. Weekend visits recommended. Combine with a visit to Tsinghua University nearby.

attractions.name: 明十三陵
attractions.description: A UNESCO World Heritage site containing the tombs of 13 Ming dynasty emperors, set in a beautiful valley with impressive stone-lined avenues.
attractions.tips: Combine with a visit to the Great Wall at Badaling. The underground palace at Dingling is the highlight.

attractions.name: 圆明园
attractions.description: The ruins of the original imperial palace and garden complex, destroyed in 1860. Features European-style architecture remains and beautiful lotus ponds.
attractions.tips: Best visited in summer when lotus flowers bloom. The ruins area tells a powerful history lesson.

attractions.name: 北京环球度假区
attractions.description: China's first Universal theme park featuring seven themed lands including the Wizarding World of Harry Potter and Kung Fu Panda Land.
attractions.tips: Book tickets well in advance. Download the Universal Beijing app. Weekdays are less crowded.

attractions.name: 鸟巢（国家体育场）
attractions.description: The iconic 2008 Olympic Games main venue, designed by Herzog & de Meuron with a distinctive steel lattice structure resembling a bird's nest.
attractions.address: 1 National Stadium South Rd, Chaoyang District
attractions.tips: Visit at night when the stadium is illuminated. Combine with nearby Water Cube and Olympic Park.

attractions.name: 水立方（国家游泳中心）
attractions.description: The stunning 2008 Olympic swimming venue, now featuring a water park. Its unique ETFE bubble facade is an architectural marvel.
attractions.tips: The water park is great for families. Visit at night for the LED light show.

attractions.name: 王府井大街
attractions.description: Beijing's most famous shopping street, spanning 1.6km with modern malls, traditional shops, and the famous Wangfujing Snack Street.
attractions.tips: Try local snacks at the snack street. The Catholic cathedral nearby is worth a visit.

attractions.name: 前门大街
attractions.description: A historic commercial street dating back to the Ming dynasty, featuring traditional Beijing shops, teahouses, and the iconic Qianmen Gate.
attractions.tips: Walk from Tiananmen Square. Try Beijing roast duck at Quanjude, the original location.

attractions.name: 孔庙和国子监
attractions.description: The second largest Confucius Temple in China and the former imperial academy where scholars studied for civil service exams.
attractions.tips: Combine with a walk through Guozijian Street and nearby Lama Temple. Audio guides available.

attractions.name: 中国园林博物馆
attractions.description: The first national-level garden museum showcasing Chinese garden art, featuring indoor and outdoor exhibits with replicas of famous gardens.
attractions.tips: Worth visiting for garden enthusiasts. Allow at least 2 hours to explore the outdoor gardens.

attractions.name: 法源寺
attractions.description: The oldest Buddhist temple in Beijing, built in 645 AD. Known for its lilac flowers in spring and peaceful atmosphere.
attractions.tips: Visit in spring when lilacs bloom. One of Beijing's hidden gems with very few tourists.

attractions.name: 中国美术馆
attractions.description: China's premier art museum housing over 100,000 works including traditional Chinese paintings, modern art, and international exhibitions.
attractions.tips: Check the exhibition schedule online. Free but reservation required. Located near Wangfujing.

attractions.name: 北京天文馆
attractions.description: China's first planetarium featuring IMAX space theater, digital sky shows, and astronomy exhibitions.
attractions.tips: Book show tickets in advance. Great for families with children. Shows in Chinese only.

attractions.name: 北京动物园
attractions.description: One of China's oldest and largest zoos, home to giant pandas, golden monkeys, and over 500 species of animals.
attractions.tips: Visit pandas early in the morning when they are most active. The aquarium is worth the extra fee.

attractions.name: 香山公园
attractions.description: A vast park on the western outskirts famous for autumn foliage, historic temples, and scenic hiking trails.
attractions.tips: Best visited in October-November for the famous red leaves. Weekends are extremely crowded in autumn.

attractions.name: 首都博物馆
attractions.description: A modern museum showcasing Beijing's history and culture from prehistoric times to the present, with an impressive bronze and porcelain collection.
attractions.tips: Free entry with reservation. Audio guides available. Modern building with excellent exhibits.

attractions.name: 爨底下村
attractions.description: A well-preserved Ming dynasty mountain village with stone houses and traditional courtyard architecture, about 90km west of Beijing.
attractions.tips: Best combined with a weekend trip. Stay overnight for the full experience. Roads can be winding.

attractions.name: 古北水镇
attractions.description: A replica water town at the foot of the Simatai Great Wall, featuring traditional architecture, hot springs, and night views of the illuminated wall.
attractions.tips: Stay overnight to see the Great Wall illuminated at night. Book hot springs in advance.

attractions.name: 国家大剧院
attractions.nameEn: National Centre for the Performing Arts
attractions.description: A stunning titanium and glass dome surrounded by water, hosting opera, ballet, concerts, and theater performances.
attractions.tips: Take a guided tour during the day. Attend an evening performance for the full experience. Book tickets early.

attractions.name: 陶然亭公园
attractions.description: A historic park famous for its classical pavilions, cherry blossoms, and peaceful lake with boat rides.
attractions.tips: Great for a relaxing afternoon. Visit in spring for cherry blossoms. Popular with local residents.

attractions.name: 八达岭野生动物园
attractions.description: A drive-through safari park at the foot of the Great Wall, featuring lions, tigers, bears, and other wildlife in semi-natural habitats.
attractions.address: Yanqing District, near Badaling Great Wall
attractions.tips: Keep car windows closed in the predator zone. Combine with a Badaling Great Wall visit.

attractions.name: 地坛公园
attractions.description: The second largest of Beijing's imperial temples, where emperors worshipped the Earth God. Features beautiful ancient cypress trees and seasonal festivals.
attractions.tips: Visit during Chinese New Year for the famous temple fair. Autumn ginkgo trees are beautiful.

attractions.name: 世界公园
attractions.description: A theme park featuring miniature replicas of over 100 famous landmarks from around the world, including the Eiffel Tower and pyramids.
attractions.tips: Great for families. Allow at least 3 hours to see everything. Cultural performances on weekends.

attractions.name: 北京自然博物馆
attractions.description: One of China's oldest natural history museums, featuring dinosaur fossils, wildlife dioramas, and interactive science exhibits.
attractions.tips: Great for children. Free admission with reservation. Book online in advance.

attractions.name: 三里屯
attractions.description: Beijing's trendiest entertainment district featuring international restaurants, bars, designer shops, and a vibrant nightlife scene.
attractions.tips: Best in the evening. Great area for international food and nightlife. Popular with expats.

attractions.name: 751D·Park
attractions.description: An industrial heritage zone next to 798 Art District, featuring converted gas tanks and factories now used for fashion shows, exhibitions, and events.
attractions.tips: Combine with a 798 Art District visit. Check the event calendar for special exhibitions.

attractions.name: 北京海洋馆
attractions.description: One of the largest inland aquariums in the world, featuring shark tanks, dolphin shows, and a tropical rainforest exhibit.
attractions.address: 18 Baishiqiao Rd, Haidian District (inside Beijing Zoo)
attractions.tips: Check dolphin show times when you arrive. Located inside Beijing Zoo. Buy combo tickets.

attractions.name: 梅兰芳纪念馆
attractions.description: The former residence of China's most famous Peking Opera performer, displaying costumes, photographs, and personal artifacts.
attractions.tips: Small but intimate museum. Best combined with nearby Shichahai and hutong walk.

restaurants.name: 新荣记
restaurants.description: Three Michelin-starred restaurant specializing in Zhejiang cuisine with exceptional seafood dishes.
dishHighlights: Steamed crab with aged Shaoxing wine

restaurants.name: 拾久
restaurants.address: 3rd Floor, 3rd Building, Sanlitun Bar St, Chaoyang District
restaurants.description: Modern Chinese fine dining with creative presentation and traditional flavors.

restaurants.name: ling-long
restaurants.address: 1-3/F, Zhao Fang Building, 8 Xinyuanli, Chaoyang District
restaurants.description: One Michelin star Taiwanese cuisine with avant-garde presentation by Chef Jason Wang.

restaurants.name: 御宝轩
restaurants.address: China World Mall, 1 Jianguomenwai Dajie, Chaoyang District
restaurants.description: Three-diamond Black Pearl Cantonese restaurant with exquisite dim sum and premium seafood.

restaurants.name: 北京厨房
restaurants.description: Popular local spot for authentic Beijing cuisine, especially duck.

restaurants.name: 全聚德
restaurants.description: Legendary Peking duck restaurant established in 1864. The definitive Beijing duck experience.

restaurants.name: 便宜坊
restaurants.description: Oldest Peking duck restaurant (established 1855) with traditional hang-roasting method.

restaurants.name: 局气
restaurants.description: Popular chain serving authentic Beijing home-style dishes in traditional atmosphere.

restaurants.name: 花家怡园
restaurants.description: Historic restaurant in a converted courtyard, known for imperial-style dishes.

restaurants.name: 利群烤鸭
restaurants.description: Famous hole-in-the-wall Peking duck restaurant favored by celebrities, no-frills authentic experience.

restaurants.name: 大董
restaurants.address: 3rd Floor, Jinbau Center, 88 Jinbao St, Dongcheng
restaurants.description: Michelin-starred restaurant revolutionizing Peking duck with modern techniques and lighter, crispier skin.

restaurants.name: 聚宝源
restaurants.description: Famous Muslim-style hotpot restaurant known for hand-cut lamb and traditional copper hotpot.

restaurants.name: 东来顺
restaurants.description: Legendary hotpot chain established in 1903, known for premium lamb and traditional铜锅涮肉.

restaurants.name: 旺顺阁鱼头泡饼
restaurants.description: Famous for fish head stew with crispy flatbread. A must-try for authentic Beijing flavor.

restaurants.name: 那家小馆
restaurants.description: Popular restaurant servingManchu and Han imperial cuisine in a traditional courtyard setting.

restaurants.name: 四季民福
restaurants.description: Modern interpretation of classic Beijing cuisine with elegant atmosphere and great views of Qianmen.

restaurants.name: 南门涮肉
restaurants.description: Popular hotpot spot near Yonghe Temple, known for fresh lamb and traditional recipe.

restaurants.name: 丰泽园
restaurants.description: Legendary Shandong cuisine restaurant established in 1930, known for excellent seafood and traditional dishes.

restaurants.name: 淮扬府
restaurants.description: Michelin-starred Huaiyang cuisine offering refined dishes from the Yangtze River Delta region.

restaurants.name: 1949-全鸭季
restaurants.description: Upscale modern Chinese restaurant in a converted factory, famous for innovative Peking duck preparations.

restaurants.name: 老吉堂
restaurants.description: Popular Shanghai cuisine restaurant known for comfort food and excellent xiaolongbao.

restaurants.name: 松鹤楼
restaurants.description: Legendary Suzhou cuisine restaurant established in 1737, known for exquisite desserts and classical dishes.

restaurants.name: 利苑酒家
restaurants.address: 3/F, Beijing Ap Hotel, 1 Jiukeshu Ave, Chaoyang
restaurants.description: Michelin-starred Cantonese restaurant known for premium dim sum and refined seafood dishes.

restaurants.name: 京季
restaurants.address: 2/F, Parkview Green, 20 Fangyuan East Rd, Chaoyang
restaurants.description: Two Michelin starred restaurant by Chef Wang Xing offering innovative Shandong cuisine.

restaurants.name: 拾城一味
restaurants.description: Popular local spot for authentic Beijing home-style dishes with modern decor.

restaurants.name: 都式烧麦馆
restaurants.description: Legendary spot for Beijing-style shaomai (steamed dumplings) since 1920s.

restaurants.name: 宫宴
restaurants.description: Upscale restaurant offering imperial court cuisine with traditional music performances.

restaurants.name: 方砖厂69号炸酱面
restaurants.description: Famous zhajiang noodles chain, Michelin-recommended for 6 consecutive years. Free extra noodles and sauce. A true Beijing staple at an unbeatable price.

restaurants.name: 护国寺小吃
restaurants.description: Iconic Beijing snack shop with over 60 years of history. Try douzhir (fermented mung bean drink), jiaoquan (fried dough rings), and wandouhuang (sweet pea cake).

restaurants.name: 姚记炒肝
restaurants.description: The most famous chaogan (fried liver) in Beijing. Thick, savory pork liver soup with garlic, served with baozi. A must-try breakfast for adventurous eaters.

restaurants.name: 老磁器口豆汁店
restaurants.description: Legendary breakfast spot for authentic Beijing douzhi (fermented mung bean drink). Not for the faint-hearted, but locals swear by it. Served with jiaoquan and pickled vegetables.

restaurants.name: 隆福寺小吃店
restaurants.description: Hidden gem serving classic Beijing snacks at rock-bottom prices. Try the tanghuoshao (baked sweet sesame bun) and miancha (millet paste). Cash only.

restaurants.name: 南城香
restaurants.description: Popular budget chain with 10-yuan meals. Free unlimited xiaomi porridge with any order. Their bazi meat (braised pork) with rice is the ultimate value meal.

restaurants.name: 超意兴
restaurants.description: Shandong-style budget restaurant chain. Self-serve model with a variety of home-cooked dishes. Full meal with meat for under 20 yuan. Popular with workers.

restaurants.name: 白魁老号饭庄
restaurants.description: Historic halal restaurant since 1780. Famous for their kaobao (baked cake) and stewed lamb. One of the oldest surviving restaurants in Beijing.

arrival.to: Beijing Capital International Airport (PEK) / Daxing International Airport (PKX)
arrival.duration: Varies by origin (8-14 hours from Europe/Americas)
arrival.tips: Daxing is newer with better facilities. PEK is closer to city center. High-speed trains from many cities.

arrival.to: Beijing Railway Stations (Beijing, Beijing West, Beijing North)
arrival.tips: High-speed trains are comfortable and punctual. Book through 12306 app or station.

metro: Metro maps available in English at stations
Line 1 (red): Runs east-west through city center
Line 2 (blue): Circular line passing major sites
Airport Express Line: Connects city center to PEK (25¥)
Daxing Line: Connects to new Daxing Airport (25¥)
First train: 05:00, Last train: 23:00 (varies by line)
Women-only cars available on some lines during peak
bus: Extensive network covering areas not served by metro
Single ride: 2¥ (flat fee for regular buses)
Night buses (夜班): 2-5¥ depending on distance
Download Beijing Transport app for real-time info
Route numbers: 1-99 for main lines, 100+ for secondary
Tips: Have exact change ready, or use transit card
taxi: Didi app works well for English speakers
Long distance trips: 2.3¥ per km after 15km
Congestion fee: May apply during rush hours in central area
Airport to city center: 100-150¥ by taxi
bike: Bike lanes common, follow traffic rules
International credit cards work with Alipay/WeChat
Designated parking zones required for shared bikes
Caution: Traffic can be chaotic, stay alert
walking: Central Beijing is walkable between major attractions
Use underground passages at major intersections
Walk on right side of sidewalks
Cross at designated crosswalks - jaywalking fines exist
Hutong areas best explored on foot
rental: Electric scooters: Require license and helmet by law
Car rental: International license accepted with translation
Driver with car: Popular option for day trips (500-800¥/day)

hotels.name: 北京华尔道夫酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Book directly for best rates. Consider weekend packages.

hotels.name: 北京王府半岛酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Check Peninsula Academy for exclusive experiences.

hotels.name: 北京千禧大酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Weekend rates often lower. Check travel sites for deals.

hotels.name: 北京希尔顿酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Hilton Honors members get better deals.

hotels.name: 北京亮马河酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Chinese travel apps often have good deals.

hotels.name: 北京瑜舍
hotels.bookingTips: Popular with design-conscious travelers. Great for nightlife.

hotels.name: 北京瑞吉酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Marriott Bonvoy members get better rates. Good for business travelers.

hotels.name: 北京香格里拉饭店
hotels.bookingTips: Best value in the luxury category. Good for tourists visiting Summer Palace.

hotels.name: 北京JW万豪酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Book directly for weekend packages. Good for combining business and leisure.

hotels.name: 北京丽晶酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best location for first-time visitors. Book rooms with city view.

hotels.name: 北京粤财jw万豪酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Good value for the location. Check for weekend specials.

hotels.name: 北京长城饭店
hotels.bookingTips: Popular with tour groups. Good for longer stays.

hotels.name: 北京希尔顿逸林酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Good base for visiting 798. Hilton Honors members save more.

hotels.name: 北京金茂万丽酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Great for shoppers. Book terrace rooms for views.

hotels.name: 北京新侨诺富特饭店
hotels.bookingTips: Best for train travelers. Good value for business.

hotels.name: 北京燕翔酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Best value near the center. Check apps for deals.

hotels.name: 北京前门建国饭店
hotels.bookingTips: Book early for best rates. Great location for tourists.

hotels.name: 北京柏悦酒店
hotels.bookingTips: Request upper floors for best views. Good for business travelers.

payment.description: Most widely accepted mobile payment in China
howToUse: Download Alipay app before arrival
Link international credit card (Visa/Mastercard accepted at many merchants)
Or open a Chinese bank account for full features
QR code payment at most merchants
tips: Many small vendors only accept Alipay/WeChat
Download before arrival as app stores may not have it
Foreign cards may not work for person-to-person transfers

payment.description: Second most popular payment method, integrated with social app
howToUse: WeChat app required
Link international card through WeChat Pay
Scan QR code to pay
Some merchants prefer WeChat over Alipay
tips: Some apps require WeChat for authentication

payment.description: Still accepted everywhere, essential for markets and small vendors
howToUse: Exchange at airport or bank
ATMs widely available (may charge fees for foreign cards)
500¥ and 100¥ notes most common
tips: Keep small bills for tips and small purchases
Bureau de change at airports offer poor rates
ICBC and Bank of China ATMs accept foreign cards

payment.description: Accepted at hotels, large restaurants, and international chains
howToUse: Visa and Mastercard widely accepted at upscale venues
May require passport verification
Exchange rates applied by your bank
tips: Call your bank to enable international transactions
Notify them of travel plans to avoid fraud alerts
Some places add foreign transaction fees

payment.description: China's domestic card network, widely accepted at ATMs and merchants
howToUse: Works at any ATM accepting UnionPay cards
Accepted at most stores, hotels, and restaurants
International cards often work with UnionPay terminals
tips: Can withdraw RMB directly from international cards
Lower fees than credit cards for cash withdrawals

payment.description: Currency exchange at banks and exchange offices
howToUse: Official exchange counters at airports
Bank branches with exchange services
Major hotels often have exchange services
tips: Airport exchange counters offer poor rates
Bank of China and ICBC give better rates

culturalTips.title: Greetings & Titles
culturalTips.content: Use 'ni hao' (hello) and 'xie xie' (thank you). Address people by surname + title (e.g., Wang Xiansheng for Mr. Wang). Handshakes are common in business settings.

culturalTips.title: Dining Etiquette
culturalTips.content: Wait to be seated. Don't stick chopsticks vertically in rice - it resembles funeral rituals. Leave some food on your plate to show you've had enough. Tipping is not expected.

culturalTips.title: Business Card Exchange
culturalTips.content: Present and receive business cards with both hands. Take a moment to read the card before putting it away respectfully.

culturalTips.title: Photography Rules
culturalTips.content: Ask permission before photographing people, especially in rural areas. Some attractions prohibit photography inside. Military areas are always off-limits.

culturalTips.title: Bargaining
culturalTips.content: Bargaining is expected at markets and small shops. Start at 30-50% of the asking price. Be friendly - it's a social activity. Walk away if you can't agree.

culturalTips.title: Number Taboos
culturalTips.content: The number 4 is considered extremely unlucky (sounds like 'death'). Avoid giving gifts in sets of 4. The number 8 is very lucky (sounds like 'prosperity'). 6 signifies smoothness. 9 signifies forever.

culturalTips.title: Color Symbolism
culturalTips.content: White and black represent mourning/death - avoid wearing to celebrations. Red represents luck and joy - appropriate for festive occasions. Yellow was imperial color - wear respectfully.

culturalTips.title: Gift Giving Taboos
culturalTips.content: Avoid giving clocks (sounds like 'death'), scissors (cutting ties), or pears (sounds like 'separation'). Umbrellas sound like 'scattering'. Wrap gifts in red - avoid white or black wrapping for celebrations.

culturalTips.title: Chopstick Etiquette
culturalTips.content: Never stick chopsticks vertically in rice (funeral ritual). Don't point with chopsticks or wave them around. Don't pass food directly chopstick-to-chopstick (used in funeral rites).

culturalTips.title: Seating Arrangements
culturalTips.content: The host faces the door; most honored guest sits to the left of host. Wait for 'please eat' before starting. The guest of honor will be offered the first serve. Leave some food to show you're satisfied.

culturalTips.title: Toasting Etiquette
culturalTips.content: Toast with both hands and lower your glass than elders/seniors. When clinking glasses, aim to be lower than those of higher rank. Finish your drink when toasting with seniors.

culturalTips.title: Personal Space
culturalTips.content: Chinese people may stand closer than Western norms. Physical contact between same genders is common and not awkward. Avoid prolonged eye contact with strangers.

culturalTips.title: Addressing People
culturalTips.content: Use full name + title for formal situations. First name alone is for close friends. 'Lao' (old) before surname shows respect for older people. 'Xiao' (little) before surname for younger people.

culturalTips.title: Business Meeting Etiquette
culturalTips.content: Arrive on time or slightly early. Exchange business cards with both hands, receive with both hands and study before putting away. Allow senior members to speak first. Avoid direct 'no' - say 'we will consider' instead.

culturalTips.title: Guanxi (Relationships)
culturalTips.content: Business in China is relationship-driven. Building trust takes time through dining, tea, and social activities. Maintaining guanxi is essential for long-term success.

culturalTips.title: Queue Culture
culturalTips.content: Lines may seem chaotic but people generally respect turns. Push ahead politely but firmly. In crowded places, state your destination clearly: 'Mai dan' (I'm paying) at restaurants, or 'Pao zhou' (noodles) at food stalls.

culturalTips.title: Public Transport Etiquette
culturalTips.content: Give seats to elderly, pregnant women, and people with children. Don't eat or drink on metro. Keep phone calls brief or on silent. Stand on right side of escalator, walk on left.

culturalTips.title: Temple Etiquette
culturalTips.content: Walk clockwise around Buddhist temples. Don't touch Buddha statues or point at them. Light incense with both hands and bow three times. Don't take photos where prohibited. Remove hats and sunglasses in temple halls.

culturalTips.title: Market Shopping Tips
culturalTips.content: Bring your own shopping bag (eco-friendly). Test electronics carefully. Check electronics carefully - ask to try before buying. Negotiate confidently but pleasantly. Many markets have wholesale minimums.

culturalTips.title: Language Tips
culturalTips.content: Learning a few Mandarin phrases is appreciated. 'Bu hao yi si' (不好意思) is useful for apologies. 'Dui bu qi' (对不起) is more formal. 'Ke yi' (可以) means 'may I?' - polite way to get attention. Use 'Zai jian' (再见) when leaving.

culturalTips.title: Beijing Dialect
culturalTips.content: The Beijing dialect (北京话) has distinct pronunciation and many colloquial expressions. 'Ye' (也) and 'zai' (再) are pronounced with a trailing 'r' sound. Learning basic phrases is appreciated.

culturalTips.title: Sandstorm Season
culturalTips.content: Beijing experiences sandstorms in spring (March-April). Check air quality indices (AQI) before going outside. Wear masks outdoors and keep windows closed during heavy sandstorms.

culturalTips.title: English Accessibility
culturalTips.content: English signage is common at major tourist attractions and hotels. However, at local markets, small restaurants, and public transport, English may be limited. Translation apps are helpful.

culturalTips.title: Beijing Dialect
culturalTips.content: The Beijing dialect (北京话) has distinct pronunciation and many colloquial expressions. 'Ye' (也) and 'zai' (再) are pronounced with a trailing 'r' sound. Learning basic phrases is appreciated.

culturalTips.title: Sandstorm Season
culturalTips.content: Beijing experiences sandstorms in spring (March-April). Check air quality indices (AQI) before going outside. Wear masks outdoors and keep windows closed during heavy sandstorms.

culturalTips.title: English Accessibility
culturalTips.content: English signage is common at major tourist attractions and hotels. However, at local markets, small restaurants, and public transport, English may be limited. Translation apps are helpful.

culturalTips.title: Beijing Roast Duck Culture
culturalTips.content: Peking duck is Beijing's most famous dish. Popular restaurants include Quanjude (est. 1864), Bianyifang (est. 1855), and Da Dong. Each has its own preparation style.

culturalTips.title: Hutong Heritage
culturalTips.content: Hutongs (胡同) are traditional alleyways forming historic Beijing neighborhoods. Walking or cycling through hutongs offers insight into traditional Beijing life.

culturalTips.title: Censorship Awareness
culturalTips.content: Internet access in Beijing is restricted. VPNs are commonly used but may be unreliable. Plan accordingly for accessing international services.

culturalTips.title: Silk Market Culture
culturalTips.content: The Silk Market (Xizhushui) and other markets offer bargaining opportunities. Start at 30-50% of asking price. Quality varies - check carefully before purchasing.

culturalTips.title: Peak Hours
culturalTips.content: Beijing traffic peaks 7-9am and 5-8pm. Public transport is recommended. Metro Line 1, 2, and 10 are most useful for tourists.

culturalTips.title: Beijing Nicknames
culturalTips.content: Locals may use Beijing-specific terms like 'dageda' (大哥大) for older men or 'jie jie' (姐姐) for women. 'Er' suffix is common: 'Beijing'er'.
